sections.paragraph:
ἄγγελος, ου, ὁ [Skt. assoc., cp. ἀγγαρεύω] ‘one who acts as an agent/courier in transmitting a message or announcement’, messenger, envoy, attendant

—a. of humans: John the Baptizer Mt 11:10; John’s agents Lk 7:24; ἀ. = spy Js 2:25.

—b. traditionally rendered angel in reference to transcendent beings: in general Mt 22:30; Ro 8:38; 1 Cor 6:3. Specifically of attendants at the service of God Mt 24:36; Lk 12:8; of God’s envoy Mt 1:20; as heavensent aide or guardian Ac 12:15; cp. Mt 18:10; as mediator Gal 3:19. Also of Satan’s envoy 2 Cor 12:7; cp. the general reference to the Slanderer’s retinue Mt 25:41.
